architecture_gray_areas.decision: Runner Control Plane is independent from offline strategy-runner-core and consumes its artifacts only as input evidence.
QMT Gateway is a service layer for lifecycle, capabilities, sessions, subscriptions, execution reports, recovery, audit, and config changes; it is not only a readonly endpoint or QMT terminal target.
Runner and Gateway communicate through domain commands/events, authorization records, idempotency keys, and audit ids; Runner must not call xtquant directly.

CP3 approval does not automatically authorize runtime, QMT, account, quote, order, submit/cancel, simulation/live, credentials, NAS, provider/lake/catalog, or Git remote write; future validation may request scoped runtime authorization.
Keep one function-named umbrella HLD for shared event/safety contracts; split Story planning by Runner, Gateway, safety, and OMS boundaries.
Gateway P0 is REST-only; SSE/WebSocket, gRPC, and FIX are deferred to later protocol CRs.

advisor_options.name: Independent Runner Control Plane + QMT Gateway Service Layer
Extend offline strategy-runner-core directly into runtime

runtime_authorization_policy: Scoped runtime authorization may be requested later with action scope, run window, redaction, rollback, and audit constraints.
